contents.text:
My name's Masoud. I am 37 years old male, 173cm and currently weigh 75.30kg. I live in Scotland and the path around me contains some slopes (so not entirely flat). I want to run 10k easily. And then work towards a half-marathon. Today is 19th July. On Wednesday (27 May 2026), I restarted running by covering a 3.27km in 20 minutes and 18 seconds (average pace of 6m13s). It probably had about about 5 meter of eleveation and it felt quite hard. But I was on fire and I did another run on the day after, Thursday 28 May 2026. This time I covered 4.06km in 22m 24s (average pace of 5m31s). This time I probably did about about 10 meters of elevation. I received some tips for my next runs which I try to follow. My plan is to run at least two or three times a week. Below is the log of all runs (including the first two runs). Each row is one run which says as the headers suggest, i.e., date, total distance, total time, average pac, list_breakdown (that is the pace for each km), and some observation or notes about how I felt. Have a look and give me my next plan. Note for my last run today (19 July) I probably shouldn't have run this fast but I didn't feel more than an RPE of 6 (as was suggested). Anyway, my next run is on Tuesday, please give me the plan including time and RPE for Tuesday

Masoud — **Tuesday plan:**

- **Run type:** Easy recovery run  
- **Total time:** **30–35 minutes**
- **RPE:** **4/10** throughout
- **Terrain:** Choose the **flattest route you can**, or take hills very gently
- **Pace:** **Conversational only**; likely around **7:00–7:40/km** on your route, but don’t chase pace

### Structure
1. **Warm-up:** 5 minutes brisk walk + easy leg swings/ankle circles
2. **Run:** 25–30 minutes very easy at **RPE 4**
3. **Optional:** If you feel excellent in the last 5 minutes, do **3 x 20 seconds relaxed quicker running** with **60–90 seconds very easy jog/walk** between
4. **Cool-down:** 5 minutes walk

### Key rule
Your last run was a bit too strong for where you are now, so **Tuesday must feel controlled and easier than Sunday**. If breathing gets laboured or hills push effort up, **slow down immediately** or take a short walk.

### Target
Finish feeling like **you could have done 10 more minutes**.
